narrative: Your balance is on track to dip below your $2,000 buffer around April 1st when your mortgage and internet bill land before your next paycheck. A $1,100 transfer from savings before March 27th keeps everything comfortable. After your mid-April paycheck you'll have solid runway — over $6,600 — through the end of the month, with a healthy $5,700 projected heading into the first week of May.

transfer_recommendation.reasoning: Your balance will fall to approximately $940 on April 1st — $1,060 below your $2,000 buffer — when the mortgage (-$2,280) and internet (-$95) hit simultaneously. Transferring $1,100 from savings before March 27th covers the gap with a small cushion.

surplus_alerts.suggested_allocation: Consider putting $2,000 toward your HELOC principal before May 1st. At ~7.5% APR, each $2,000 payment saves roughly $12.50/month in interest going forward.

risk_flags: Apr 1–3: balance drops to $940, which is $1,060 below your $2,000 buffer — transfer from savings recommended before Mar 27
HELOC balance of $48,200 at ~7.5% APR is costing roughly $300/month in interest; accelerating paydown would meaningfully improve long-term cash flow
seasonal_notes: April historically runs $150–200 higher on dining and entertainment based on your spending patterns — worth building in a little extra flexibility for discretionary spending this month.
